observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to carry out the following measures:
Trace and identify the reconciling items pertaining to the cash in bank accounts;
Ensure the complete preparation and timely submission of the BRS; and
Examine the causes of the negative balance of cash in bank accounts of the GF and TF, and make the necessary adjustments.
We further recommended that Management, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer, resolve the discrepancies in their respective records, make the necessary adjustments, and consistently reconcile the balances of both records.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the noted discrepancy in the above-mentioned reciprocal accounts and thereafter, effect the necessary adjusting entries thereon to correct the ending balances of these affected accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to cancel the unclaimed stale checks and to regularly monitor all outstanding checks and issue written notices to the payees a month before these become stale.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry debiting the Cash in Bank and crediting Accounts Payable and Prior Year’s Adjustment for stale checks less than two years and those more than two years, respectively as follows:
Cash in Bank			₱60,469.25
Accounts Payable			₱9,925.25
Prior Period Adjustment			₱50,544.00
#
During the Exit Conference, the Municipal accountant said that no cancelled checks were forwarded from the Municipal Treasurer so that the necessary adjustments can be made.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the year-end balances reflected in their respective records of cash in local treasury to ensure accuracy and consistency.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant with the duly certified list of all taxpayers at the beginning of the year showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year as basis in setting-up the RPT and SET Receivables in the books for proper accounting.
We also recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Accountant set-up RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year based on the certified list of taxpayers pursuant to Section 20 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs and maintain the corresponding subsidiary ledgers to monitor tax collections and delinquencies.
We recommended and Management, through the HRMO and the LDRRMO, agreed to inquire on the status of the appeal with the CSC, and thereafter take appropriate action on the status of the appointment.

We recommended that Management:
Formally request an extension of the validity of the funds from the DBM to prevent the funds from lapsing and to ensure that they can still be utilized for their intended purpose;
Return the unexpended balance from completed projects amounting to ₱53,764.34 to the National Treasury; and
Take immediate steps to comply with the mandated posting and reporting requirements for the LGSF-SBDP as stipulated in Item 5.0 of LBC No. 151.

We recommended that Management, through the Municipal GAD Focal Person, provide a written explanation for the disbursements made from the GAD Fund that were not directly attributable to the promotion of gender equality and women’s empowerment.
We further recommended to ensure that all GAD-related programs, projects and activities are carried out efficiently and in strict adherence to the provisions of RA No. 9710 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2014-1.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the GFPS to undertake the following:
Prepare the three-year GAD Agenda in order for the Municipality to have its strategic framework and plan for gender mainstreaming and achieving women empowerment and gender equality; and
Henceforth, review the alignment of the annual GPB with the GAD Agenda.

We recommended that Management develop and implement policy guidelines to standardize wage rates for the JO workers of the Municipality to ensure that JOs receive fair and equitable compensation in line with their assigned tasks.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and Municipal Budget Officer to collaborate to develop a holistic and well-coordinated plan that addresses the different barriers to project implementation. This plan should aim to ensure that priority projects are timely carried out with the view of promoting and enhancing efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the utilization of government resources in compliance with the provisions set forth in DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 2020-1.

We recommended that Management, through the Municipal Accountant:
cause the immediate remittance of the unremitted contributions and loan payments amounting to ₱438,995.52, to avoid incurrence of penalties and surcharges and to enable the member, including beneficiaries to avail of the benefits from the GSIS; and
come up with appropriate measures to ensure that the monthly withheld contributions and loan payments are remitted promptly and accurately

We recommended that Management, through the Municipal Accountant, ensure that the exact amount of withheld contributions are remitted to PhilHealth Office to avoid over-payment.
We also recommended to file a request to the PhilHealth Office to offset the overpaid contributions to the succeeding remittances. This would prevent the over-remitted amount from being deemed an unnecessary expenditure that might warrant the issuance of disallowance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through the Municipal Engineer:
fast track the submission of contracts with complete set of supporting documents to facilitate the immediate conduct of the auditorial and technical review of the projects; and
for completed projects, submit the complete set of documents required in the same COA circular for technical inspection

We recommended that cash advances of the LGU shall be liquidated in accordance with the provisions of the aforementioned circular. The concerned officers/employees with unliquidated cash advances shall effect immediate settlement of the same to avoid the sanctions imposed under existing laws and regulations specifically under Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code for failure to render account.
We also recommended that demand letters be sent immediately to the accountable officers especially those that are no longer connected with the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the General Services Officer, prioritize the registration or titling of these parcels of land in the name of the Municipal Government of Aloguinsan to ensure ownership and  to fully protect the interest of the Municipality in compliance with COA Circular No. 92-386. In alternative, we recommended for the creation of Land Titling Committee to intensify/expedite the titling efforts of the municipality

We recommended and Management, through the Municipal Accountant, agreed to adhere to the prescribed timeline under Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996, on the submission of the BRS to enable timely verification and communication of audit results, particularly when deficiencies are identified.
Submit the Certificate of Time Deposit and its accompanying bank statement for the CIB-LCTD account.
observations.reason: It was confirmed during CY 2023 exit conference that the CIB-LCTD is not a Time Deposit account but rather a Special Savings account in the bank; hence adjustment is needed so as not to mislead the user of the financial statements. Direct the RHU to make a written representation addressed to the CPHO to clarify the issuance of vaccines and ancillary medical supplies without unit cost so that these could be recorded and fairly valued in the books of the Municipality as inventory as at December 31, 2022.

RHU to furnish a copy of the Inventory of COVID-19 vaccine report to the Accounting Office for the latter to further verify, and thereafter take-up the verified balance in the Municipality’s books of accounts to adjust the balances of both the inventory and donation accounts.

Henceforth, both offices should observe proper coordination in order to prevent similar omissions/ errors in their future operations considering that the vaccination is still a continued priority program in 2023.
Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to procure the consultancy services pursuant to RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations.
Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant to ensure sufficient and complete documentation of consultancy payments pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001.
Management to revisit the functions of the legal counsel to be hired alongside the requirement of COA Circular No. 98-002 dated June 9, 1998 and ensure compliance thereof.

LDRRMO submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month and include in the report all information required in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to enable the COA and other offices concerned to conduct evaluation of the LDRRM Fund utilization on time.

Henceforth, Management through the Municipal Treasurer require all the Accountable Officers to immediately prepare the RAAF in accordance with the above-mentioned Manual on a monthly basis. The report should also strictly follow the prescribed format as provided in Annex 42 of the same Manual.

Require the Municipal Engineer to ensure that the required billboards/signboards for the projects are installed and contain the relevant information of the project, for notification and/or publicity purposes, in accordance with the guidelines set forth in the Circular.
Submit a written justification as to the composition of the members of the LSB and henceforth create their LSB pursuant to Section 98(b) (3) of RA No. 7160.

A three-year program for the strategic allocation of the SEF and submit the Local Board Resolutions for CY 2017, School Improvement Plans, and Division Education Development Plan for auditorial review.

We recommended the LDRRM Council to follow the format in Annex-A and the specific guidelines in the preparation of LDRRM Investment Plan provided in COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We also recommended to consider in the LDRRMFIP not only the current year appropriation but as well as the unexpended balances from previous years in order to maximize the utilization of the Municipality for projects and activities that are geared towards disaster preparedness, prevention and mitigation.
observations.managementAction: The Management on their justification letter submitted on March 12, 2021, affirmed to use the prescribed format on the next year’s budget.
observations.reason: The non-incorporation in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) of the unexpended balance of prior years’ Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) is reiterated in Part II, Item No. 10 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that LDRRM Officer refund the excess salary received and submit to the Auditor copies of official receipts as proof of payment thereof. 

Furthermore, we also recommended that Management strictly adhere to the salary schedule allowed for LDRRMO to 4th-class Municipalities as provided in JMC No. 2014-01 dated April 4, 2014.
observations.managementAction: The Management has cited in the subsection, 5.6 of the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-01 that if an LGU is financially capable and has not exceeded the PS limitation, the Local Sanggunian may elevate the organization and staffing of LDRRMO.
observations.reason: The Management’s comment last year to request clarification on the interpretation of JMC No. 2014-01 from DBM was not realized; hence, the salary of the appointed LDRRM Officer was not justified.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should review and reconcile discrepancies in the accounts in order to come up with the proper adjustments to correct and validate the actual balances of the inter-agency payable accounts.
observations.managementAction: The present Municipal Accountant had prepared monthly reconciliation of these accounts except for those prior to his appointment.
observations.reason: Proper reconciliation was still not timely effected due to the slow movement and processing of documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management enjoin the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the PPE book balances with Property Records maintained by General Services Officer or Municipal Treasurer’s Office. Further, instruct the General Services Officer or Municipal Treasurer and Heads of other Offices to coordinate closely with the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the accountability of employees and other details of the various PPEs are properly identified and documented in their respective offices.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to trace the inclusions in the reciprocal accounts and to examine if it is necessary to remain as an Intra-Agency Receivable or as an Intra-Agency Payable; and further instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the adjustments, if warranted, so as to reflect the correct and accurate reciprocal balances.

We recommended and Management agreed that utmost efforts be exerted by the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to identify the cause/s, review, analyze and reconcile the abnormal balances so that appropriate action can be made to rectify the errors causing those abnormal balances.

We recommended to strictly enforce the terms and conditions provided for each contract agreement. We also recommended to provide corresponding actions in the non-completion of the mentioned projects such as but not limited to the forfeiture of performance security, payment of liquidated damages or even termination of the Contract as provided in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended Management to always ensure that priority projects are timely carried out and the fund is optimally utilized with the view of promoting and enhancing efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the utilization of government resources in compliance with the provisions set forth in DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 and 2020-1.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit certification from the Municipal Treasurer that the investment is considered as an idle fund including details on the specific sources and composition of the amount invested.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Disposal Committee to comply with the mandatory procedures in the disposal of all obsolete and unserviceable properties pursuant to the provisions of Sections 379, 380 and 381 of RA 7160.

We recommended and Management agreed through the LDRRM Council to follow the format in Annex-A and the specific guidelines in the preparation of LDRRM Investment Plan provided in COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
We also recommended and Management agreed to consider in the LDRRMFIP not only the current year appropriation but also the unexpended balances from prior years in order to maximize the utilization of the Municipality for projects and activities that are geared towards disaster preparedness, prevention and mitigation.

We recommended that Management submit explanation or justification on the noted discrepancies or otherwise require the responsible persons having participation in the transactions to refund the discrepancy noted in the amount of ₱291,764.89 & ₱257,991.44, respectively of the two above-mentioned projects.
We recommended and Management agreed to comply with Section 8 of RA 11313 and Section 9 of its IRR to ensure enforcement of the provisions of the Safe Spaces Act.

We recommended the Management to prospectively adhere and comply with the correct withholding of tax for each payment made to its supplier or contractor pursuant to Section 2.57.2 (J) of the Bureau of Internal Revenue RR No. 2-98 as renumbered and amended by RR 11-2018.

We recommended that Management prepare the Pre-Inspection report on the damaged properties and Post- Inspection report of the work accomplished as well as the Waste Materials Report to determine the necessity of the disposal and to determine if indeed certain parts were replaced. The replaced parts should be kept for future inspection by the Auditor based in the Waste Material Report. Property Index Card must also be maintained by the accounting unit to enable the monitoring of all cost of repairs incurred.

We further recommended that Management strictly comply in attaching the required documents. Failure to do so will be a ground for disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Management had instructed the Accountable officers to submit the required supporting documents as soon as possible and carefully review all transaction documents to avoid further AOM’s relative to this transaction.
observations.reason: The required documents submitted on March 18, 2022 were in order.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct those responsible for the procurement to submit a written justification for the noted over-priced medical supplies to avoid disallowance in audit. 
 
We also recommended that Management must be guided by the prices of Essential Emergency Medicines and Medical Devices set by DOH during the COVID-19 Health Crisis to ensure that the government achieves the best value for money in sourcing essential medical goods.
observations.managementAction: The Management had submitted a justification letter on March 12, 2021 that they were actually hesitant on the procurement of the said medical supplies, however, proceeded with the procurement due to exigency caused by the COVID-19 pandemic.
observations.reason: The Audit Team acknowledged the LGU’s justification considering that the procurement happened on the onset of the COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) comply with the mandatory posting of APP, NOA and other information relative to all emergency procurement under Bayanihan Act on the GPPB Portal as mandated in GPPB Circular 2020-01 dated April 6, 2020.

We also recommended that the BAC submit explanation/justification on the non-posting of the emergency procurement in the GPPB Portal.
observations.managementAction: The Management had submitted a justification letter acknowledging their lapses of not posting all the procurements covered under the Bayanihan Act as prescribed in the GPPB provisions and submitted the same required documents.
observations.reason: The submitted documents showed screenshots that the procurement reports of the Municipality were now posted accordingly in the GPPB- Emergency Procurement Portal as well as in the PhilGEPS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit justification for undertaking the Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act without complying with the requirements of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020. Also, we reminded management to be kept updated with the issuances of the GPPB as the governing board for all the procurement activities of the government.
observations.managementAction: The Management had submitted a justification letter on March 12, 2021 that the LGU inadvertently overlooked the required inclusion of a warranty clause and the use of the required Omnibus Sworn Statement (OSS) for all the emergency procurements covered under the Bayanihan Act. However, they used the usual Omnibus Sworn Statement (OSS) as prescribed by the revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.reason: The Audit Team acknowledged the LGU’s justification.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management submit a written justification on the procurement of reordered goods in excess of the unit price of the original contract and the non-compliance of the required statement in the NOA on repeat orders as required under GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020.
observations.managementAction: The Management had submitted a justification letter on March 12, 2021 that only Negotiated Procurement-Emergency Cases was applied on all their COVID-19 procurements under the Bayanihan Act and that they inadvertently overlooked the provisions of Special Repeat Orders in the mentioned circular; hence, the non-compliance with the required statement in the NOA. Furthermore, the Management failed to notice the procurement of reordered goods with different unit price.
observations.reason: Complied accordingly with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management explain in writing why the unutilized amount of ₱7,745,783.00 of the BGCM which could have been used to further respond to the COVID-19 emergency was reverted back to the National Treasury even if the State of Calamity has not yet been lifted by the President of the Philippines contrary to Item 3.9 of LBC Circular No. 125 dated April 7, 2020.
observations.managementAction: The Management had submitted a justification letter on March 12, 2021 that the LGU only abided with the Regional Inter-Agency Task Force (RIATF)-7 Resolution No. 21 to fully utilize the Bayanihan Grant before September 15, 2020, the date even before the President issued the extension of the state of calamity for a period of one year on September 16, 2020 (Proclamation No. 1021).
observations.reason: Submitted copies of the RIATF-7 Resolution and Proclamation No. 1021 have proved their claim.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management establish an Internal Audit Unit to ensure sound internal control system and improve agency operations pursuant to the aforementioned provisions.
observations.managementAction: The Sanggunian Bayan already created the position of Internal Auditor who will then comprise the Internal Audit Service of the LGU.
observations.reason: The position of Internal Auditor II, with Salary Grade 15 has been filled in by Ms. Mitchel P. Ignario with Plantilla Item No. 57 and was assumed to duty on September 1, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their balances. Further, instruct the Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer and other Section Chiefs to coordinate closely with the Municipal Accountant regarding the nature and other details of various property and equipment used by the respective offices.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality’s Inventory team had reconciled with the accounting records and had identified that some booked PPEs were missing and/or could not be located and transaction documents related to these PPEs were not found.
observations.reason: The reliability and accuracy of the consolidated Property, Plant and Equipment account is reiterated in Part II, Item No. 1, of this report wherein there was still a material unreconciled difference amounting to ₱5,038,907.67 as of December 31, 2021 between the accounting records and the RPCPPE.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor, as the Chairperson of the Municipal Development Council, to submit an explanation/justification for non-implementation of priority projects for CY 2020.

We also recommended to assess the Municipality’s current procedure in the utilization of 20% Development Fund so that all the bottlenecks in the process can be identified and remedial measures be adopted to ensure that priority projects are timely carried out and the fund is optimally utilized with the view of effecting efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the disposition of government resources.
observations.managementAction: The subject funding from the 20% Development Fund has already been realigned to another object of expenditures to facilitate the fund utilization. A Resolution to this effect was already passed by the MDC with the approval from the Sangguniang Bayan.
observations.reason:
Based on the submitted Quarterly Report on Government Projects/Programs/ Activities of the LGU, the realignment of some of the projects under the 20% DF for CY 2020 have been completed except for the project “Road Concreting of Barangay Angilan to Barangay Punay Rd.” with an approved budget of ₱5,194,355.93 that was suspended due to Typhoon Odette.
Further, the non-utilization of current appropriations for 20% Development Fund is reiterated in Part II, Item No. 7, of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that detailed subsidiary ledgers be maintained for the Due to LGU, Due to NGA, with an aging schedule of accounts prepared periodically and utilized as a monitoring tool for the settlement of consequent claims and the implementation of projects and procurement activities as well.
observations.managementAction: The present Municipal Accountant has prepared reconciliation of the account to maintain accurate subsidiary ledgers with the exclusion of those prior to his assumption.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant maintains the required subsidiary ledgers but these were not yet updated to consider the previous accounts handled by the former accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that cash advances of the LGU shall be liquidated in accordance with the provisions of the aforementioned circular. The concerned officers/employees with unliquidated cash advances shall effect immediate settlement of the same in order to avoid the sanctions imposed under existing laws and regulations specifically under Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code for failure to render account.

We also recommended that demand letters be sent immediately to the accountable officers especially those that are no longer connected with the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters had been served to Accountable Officer to settle their accounts. On the other hand, the Management has sent a letter request for write-off for the unsettled accounts accrued to the deceased and dismissed AOs.
observations.reason: The long outstanding unliquidated cash advances is reiterated in Part II, Item No. 6 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should take the necessary and appropriate actions for the failure of the accountable officer to liquidate their cash advances as provided for under COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Demand letter had been served to settle the accounts.
observations.reason: The status of such cash advances remained the same as no liquidation was effected by the accountable officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Social Welfare Development Officer send demand letters to the 190 ineligible beneficiaries for the immediate refund of the illegally received subsidy totaling P1,140,000.00.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters had been served to ineligible beneficiaries for the immediate refund of their illegally received subsidy.
observations.reason: Out of the ₱1,140,000 subsidy received by the 190 ineligible beneficiaries, only ₱22,000 have been collected by the Municipality as of December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: The Accountant should undertake a detailed reconciliation/analysis of the accounts and investigate causes of the error/discrepancies for the purpose of booking up the necessary adjustments to arrive at the correct balances of the accounts.
observations.managementAction: The present Municipal Accountant has prepared reconciliation of the account to maintain accurate subsidiary ledgers with the exclusion of those prior to his assumption.
observations.reason:
As of December 31, 2021, the account “Other Payables” in the General Fund remained abnormal with debit balance.

This is reiterated in Part II, Item No. 3 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the current General Services Officer (GSO-Designate) of the Municipality ensure at all times that items delivered are in accordance with the quality, specifications and quantity provided in the purchase order or contract.

We recommended also the Management to justify the said violation.
observations.managementAction: The Management on their justification letter, committed to correct their failure next time and make the accountable officers (TWG & Inspection Officer) be more responsible in verifying and accepting delivered items in accordance with the quality, specification, and quantity prescribed by the End User.
observations.reason: Complied accordingly with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a reconciliation report of reciprocal accounts and to take up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to ensure that all reconciling items are resolved and/or adjusted on a timely basis and perform constant review and appraisal of transactions to deter error and omissions.
observations.managementAction: The present Municipal Accountant has prepared reconciliation of the account to maintain accurate subsidiary ledgers with the exclusion of those prior to his assumption.
observations.reason: The unreconciled reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds is reiterated in Part II, Item No. 2 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as the head of the agency, direct the Municipal Treasurer to generate computerized copies (excel format) of the cashbooks as well as of the report of collections and deposits from CYs 2013 to present and furnish the team with the softcopies of the same in order to facilitate our verification of the discrepancies.

We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant regularly reconcile the cashbook balance with the accounting records so as to timely effect the necessary corrections and consistently harmonize the balances of both records.
observations.managementAction: The Management had submitted a reconciliation report for CY 2020 & 2021.
observations.reason:
The submitted reconciliation report showed that the cashbook balance maintained by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office has been reconciled with the accounting records with corresponding proforma entries for adjustments made by the Municipal Accountant. However, verification of the said reconciliation could not be determined since softcopies of report of collections and deposits since CY 2013 were not provided.

Moreover, reconciliation of cashbook balance was only conducted at the end of the year and not on a regular basis or at least quarterly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit an explanation or justification on the noted discrepancies or otherwise require the responsible persons having participation in the transaction to refund the discrepancy noted in the amount of ₱30,169.98.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer on their justification letter, explained that the volume and quantity of items indicated in the original Program of Work and Plan were met as they reviewed and visited the site on February 10, 2021.
observations.reason: The team will be sending a request to the Technical Services Office (TSO) to re-evaluate the report submitted by the Agency to reconcile said report against the inspection report of the TSO.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the municipality submit a written justification as to the composition of the members of the Local School Board and henceforth create their Local School Board pursuant to Section 98(b) (3) of Republic Act No. 7160.

We also recommended that a three-year program for the strategic allocation of the Special Education Fund and submit the Local Board Resolutions for CY 2017, School Improvement Plans, and Division Education Development Plan for auditorial review. (AOM No. 2018-007 dated 2/15/2018)

The Municipal Mayor had created and constitutionalized a Local School Board of the Municipality of Aloguinsan by issuing the Executive Order No. 17, series of 2021.

However, the three-year program for the strategic allocation of the Special Education Fund, Local Board Resolutions, and School Improvement Plan were not prepared.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management (a) create an inventory committee to
conduct an actual physical count of all the properties of the  agency  in
accordance with Section 124, Volume I of the  New  Government  Accounting
System (NGAS) for LGUs, (b)  submit  Report  on  the  Physical  Count  of
Property, Plant  and  Equipment  (RPCPPE)  and  a  reconciled  report  on
physical inventory of the PPE Account  to  the  auditor  not  later  than
January 31 of each year, and (c) to instruct the Municipal Accountant  to
maintain PPE Ledger Cards and reconcile every year the physical inventory
report.

We recommended that cash advances of the LGU shall be liquidated in
accordance with  the  provisions  of  the  aforementioned  circular.  The
concerned officers/employees with unliquidated cash advances shall effect
immediate settlement of the same in order to avoid the sanctions  imposed
under existing laws and regulations specifically under Article 218 of the
Revised Penal Code for failure to render account.


We also recommended that demand letters be sent immediately to  the
accountable officers especially those that are no longer  connected  with
the LGU.

We recommended the Municipal Development Council  to  optimize  the
utilization of the 20% Development Fund  and  ensure  that  projects  are
implemented fully within the time frame.


For projects that are still unimplemented  and  on-going,  positive
efforts should be made to fast track  the  completion  of  the  same  and
ensure close monitoring of its  implementation  in  accordance  with  the
Municipal Development Plan.

We  recommended  that  Management  adheres  to  the   policy   that
government funds and property should be fully  protected  and  conserved,
and irregular, unnecessary,  excessive,  extravagant  and  unconscionable
expenditures or uses of funds and property should  be  prevented.  It  is
also recommended to  submit  the  completely  filled-out  documents  duly
authorized and signed by the proper officials as noted above. Lastly,  it
is recommended to purchase brand new units for better service as it could
also save government funds.

We recommended that Management (a) initiate  steps  to  comply  and
implement the supply and property management as embodied in Chapter 7  of
the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Volume  I,  (b)
require the GSO and other custodians of supplies to undertake the  actual
physical inventory every semester and to submit inventory reports to  the
Auditor; and (c) enjoin all stock custodians and the Accounting Office to
perform the reconciliation of the inventories with the recorded  balances
in the books.

We recommended that management direct the LDRRM Officer to prepare and timely submit the LGU’s Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF certified correct by the Municipal Accountant in prescribed form on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, through the LDRRMC and LDC, to the COA Auditor of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 | The required monthly reports of Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF were already submitted to COA Office through email since June of 2019. Eventually, the said report was regularly submitted. The original copies of these reports will be submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit the GAD Accomplishment Report duly endorsed by the DILG with all the required attachments enumerated in Section 7 of the JMC to enable us to properly evaluate whether the planned programs/projects/activities and the GAD reported utilizations were all gender responsive and addresses the gender issues of the Municipality’s constituents.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 | The Management directed the concerned personnel to submit the required copy of GAD Accomplishment Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit copies of all perfected government contracts, purchase orders and all supporting documents for the procurement of goods & services within five (5) working days from execution/issuance pursuant with COA Circular No. 2009-001 to facilitate timely auditorial and technical review thereof. Unjustifiable failure to submit the same shall subject the officials and employees concerned to administrative disciplinary action.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 | The Management took notice to COA Circular No. 2009-001. Thus, effective this Calendar Year of 2020, all the copies of all perfected contracts, purchase orders and other supporting documents for the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects will be submitted to COA within the required period from the execution/issuance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: require the concerned drivers to henceforth prepare the duly accomplished and approved Driver’s Trip Tickets; submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption to avoid disallowance in audit; and henceforth, submit not later that every 10th of the succeeding month the above-mentioned Reports.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 | The Management had instructed the concerned officer to accomplish the required documents and regularly submit all the copies of which to COA auditors on a timely basis. For the year 2019, all these documents are being attached to the respective vouchers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management execute a one year contract consolidating its procurement requirements for the supply of petroleum, oil and lubricants products the value of which shall not exceed the approved budget for year in compliance with COA Circular 92-386 and the rules and regulations on procurement under R.A. No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 | The Management took cognizance to COA Circular No. 92-386. In fact in the years 2019 and 2020, contracts between the LGU and the Petroleum, Oil and Lubricant suppliers were being executed. Copies of which has been submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit: the documents on the Small Value Procurement as required in Item II, Appendix A, Annex “H” on Revised IRR, R.A. 9184; and explanation why the subject disbursements appeared to have been split into smaller quantities even though requisition and/or activity(ies) were conducted almost at the same time.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 | The procurement of meals and snacks on various activities appeared to have been split and proceeded into smaller quantities considering the absence of any supplier who is Platinum in registration in our area. However, the management took compliance on the requirements needed on the small value procurements. The required documents were already submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management shall submit the following documents to support the disbursements, otherwise, these shall be disallowed in audit: Letter of Authority from his/her office; Memorandum of Agreement stipulating the allowances and other benefits to be granted to the detailed employee; and Daily Time Record and Accomplishment Reports for the period.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 | The required supporting documents were already attached in the respective vouchers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Accountant effect the necessary correction in the financial statement where it occurred following the requirements provided in PPSAS 3 including such disclosure of certain items in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 | The Management is still waiting for the issuance of LGAM.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that cash advances shall be liquidated in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.  The Disbursing Officer should submit a payroll lists duly acknowledged and signed by the winners and judges to avoid disallowance in audit; and to adhere strictly on the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 as well as the Implementing Rules and Regulation of Republic Act 10632.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 | Compliance has been submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management see to it that tardiness and undertime incurred by job order personnel must be deducted accordingly from their respective pay/wages. We also recommended to compute the equivalent amount of tardiness and undertime and direct the responsible official/s to refund such amounts as these were made in payment of employees for the times that they were actually not working.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 | Compliance has been submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management to strictly observe the prescribed procurement process as outlined in RA 9184 and its revised IRR in line with the government’s commitment to promote good governance and its effort to adhere to the principles of transparency, accountability, equity, efficiency and economy in procurement. We recommended as well to submit justification as to the mentioned observations and submit the required supporting documents for further verification purposes.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 | Compliance has been submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the management to create an inventory committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of all the properties of the municipality every year and submit a properly filled up and signed inventory report to the local chief executive copy furnished the office of the auditor. Reconciliation with accounting records shall then be made and the municipal accountant shall then prepare adjusting entries, if necessary, to come up with a reliable PPE account balances in the financial statements. Also, property records such as property cards and ledger cards must at all times be maintained by the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant, respectively, to facilitate reconciliation with the physical inventory and immediate verification of discrepancies. It is further recommended that the Inventory Committee should follow a system/procedures, an example of which is outlined in Sec. 491 of the GAAM, Vol. 1, so that annual inventory-taking will really take place and completed within the year and the report duly reconciled with accounting records to establish the existence, condition and valuation of the PPE balances presented in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 | The Inventory committee is still on process of reconciling items in Physical Inventory of PPE with Accounting records since some PPE records were recorded in Lump-sum amount.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should take the necessary and appropriate actions for the failure of the accountable officer to liquidate their cash advances as provided for under COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 | Demand letters have been served to the former Mayor directing her to settle the obligation. The management did not receive any comments and reply from the former. Hence, the matter will be referred to the Executive Assistant for Legal Affairs for appropriate action.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipality ensure that the 20% development funds be optimally utilized to help attain desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes and see to it that the programs projects funded out of the funds are in accordance with the provisions of the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum provisions of Joint Circular 1-11 for the programming for development project.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 | Additional engineering staff were hired in order to cover up the delay in the implementation of the projects. The management takes the responsibility of ensuring that all of the pending projects will be implemented soonest in the best interest of the public.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that detailed subsidiary ledgers be maintained for the Due to LGU, Due to NGA, with an aging schedule of accounts prepared periodically and utilized as a monitoring tool for the settlement of consequent claims and the implementation of projects and procurement activities as well.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 | Reconciliation of the account is still on process since there were still details of accounts that were not established considering the age of accounts. The Accountant commits to take appropriate action recommended by the Auditors.

observations.recommendation: The Accountant should undertake a detailed reconciliation/analysis of the accounts and investigate causes of the error/discrepancies for the purpose of booking up the necessary adjustments to arrive at the correct balances of the accounts.
observations.managementAction: 2011 AAR | Liability accounts were still on the process of reconciliation, due to difficulties in locating those documents that will be needed in reconciling the accounts. The Accountant commits to take appropriate action recommended by the Auditors.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management see to it that the granting of cash advances shall be supported with payroll and Accountant’s Certification that previous cash advance was fully liquidated and accounted for in the books in compliance with COA Circular 97-002 and COA Circular 201-001.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management see  to  it  that  the  granting  of  cash
   advances shall be supported with payroll and  Accountant’s  Certification
   that previous cash advance was fully liquidated and accounted for in  the
   books in compliance with COA Circular 97-002 and COA Circular 201-001.

We recommended that  management  submit  copies  of  all  perfected
   government contracts, purchase orders and all  supporting  documents  for
   the procurement of goods & services within five  (5)  working  days  from
   execution/issuance pursuant with COA Circular No. 2009-001 to  facilitate
   timely auditorial and technical review thereof. Unjustifiable failure  to
   submit the same shall subject the officials and  employees  concerned  to
   administrative disciplinary action.

We recommended the management to direct the Municipal Accountant to
   prepare a reconciliation report of reciprocal accounts and to take up the
   necessary adjustments in  the  books  of  accounts  to  ensure  that  all
   reconciling items are resolved and/or adjusted  on  a  timely  basis  and
   perform constant review and appraisal of transactions to deter error  and
   omissions.

We recommended that management:
     a. require the  concerned  drivers  to  henceforth  prepare  the  duly
        accomplished and approved Driver’s Trip Tickets;
     b. submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels  and  Monthly  Report
        of Fuel Consumption to avoid disallowance in audit; and
     c. henceforth, submit not later than  every  10th  of  the  succeeding
        month the above-mentioned Reports.

We  recommended  that  management  execute  a  one  year   contract
   consolidating its procurement requirements for the supply  of  petroleum,
   oil and lubricants products the value  of  which  shall  not  exceed  the
   approved budget for year in compliance with COA Circular 92-386  and  the
   rules and regulations on procurement under R.A. 9184.

We recommended that Management submit:

   1. the documents on the Small Value Procurement as required in  Item  II,
      Appendix A, Annex “H” on Revised IRR, R.A. 9184; and
   2. explanation why the subject disbursements appeared to have been  split
      into smaller quantities even though requisition  and/or  activity(ies)
      were conducted almost of the same time.

We recommended  that  Municipal  Accountant  effect  the  necessary
   correction in the financial statement where  it  occurred  following  the
   requirements provided in IPSAS 3 including  such  disclosure  of  certain
   items in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended  that  management  submit  the  GAD  Accomplishment
   Report duly endorsed by  the  DILG  with  all  the  required  attachments
   enumerated in Section 7 of the JMC to  enable  us  to  properly  evaluate
   whether the planned programs/projects/activities  and  the  GAD  reported
   utilizations were all gender responsive and addresses the  gender  issues
   of the Municipality’s constituents.

We recommended that management direct the LDRRM Officer  to  prepare  and
   timely submit the LGU’s Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization  of
   DRRMF certified correct by the Municipal Accountant in prescribed form on
   or before the 15th day after the end of each month,  through  the  LDRRMC
   and LDC, to the COA Auditor of the municipality.

observations.recommendation:
Create an inventory committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of all the properties of the municipality every year and submit a properly filled up and signed inventory report to the local chief executive copy furnished the office of the auditor.  Reconciliation with accounting records shall then be made and the municipal accountant shall then prepare adjusting entries, if necessary, to come up with a reliable PPE account balances in the financial statements.  Also, property records such as property cards and ledger cards must at all times be maintained by the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant, respectively, to facilitate reconciliation with the physical inventory and immediate verification of discrepancies.
It is further recommended that the Inventory Committee should follow a system/procedures, an example of which is outlined in Sec. 491 of the GAAM, Vol. 1, so that annual inventory-taking will really take place and completed within the year and the report duly reconciled with accounting records to establish the existence, condition and valuation of the PPE balances presented in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management has directed for the creation of an inventory committee and submit an inventory report thereof.
